title: History of Tom Jones, a Foundling
author: Fielding, Henry
summary: The History of Tom Jones, a Foundling follows the upbringing, misadventures, and romantic pursuits of Tom Jones, an amiable foundling raised by the hospitable Mr Allworthy. Surrounded by a comic ensemble — Bridget Allworthy, Mrs Deborah Wilkins, Partridge, Mr Square, Mr Thwackum, Sophia Western, Squire Western, Molly Seagrim and others — Tom’s life traces schooling, duels of honor, amorous entanglements, mistaken identities, flights to inns and cities (Gloucester, Upton), and courtly and domestic disputes. Fielding intersperses digressions on morality, satire of critics, legal and clerical customs, and reflections on love, conscience, and human folly in a sprawling comic social panorama.

characters.name: Tom Jones
characters.personality: good-natured, prone to youthful indiscretions and romantic impulse
characters.lifespan: protagonist throughout the narrative; survives through the events described (fate not specified in excerpt)

characters.name: Mr Allworthy (Squire Allworthy)
characters.personality: hospitable, paternal, generous
characters.lifespan: guardian of Tom; presides over many family events (fate not specified in excerpt)

characters.name: Miss Bridget Allworthy
characters.personality: kind, condescendingly gracious; central to domestic plots
characters.lifespan: marries Captain Blifil (Book II); further fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Mrs Deborah Wilkins
characters.personality: decent, moralizing, outspoken on bastards
characters.lifespan: active in parish scenes (fate not specified in excerpt)

characters.name: Jenny Jones
characters.personality: depicted with learning difficulties and discouragements
characters.lifespan: background character; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Captain / Master Blifil
characters.personality: antagonistic foil in domestic and educational scenes
characters.lifespan: marries Miss Bridget Allworthy (Book II); further fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Partridge (Mr Partridge)
characters.personality: schoolmaster, awkward, comic; subject of a trial
characters.lifespan: tried for incontinency; outcome not detailed in excerpt

characters.name: Mrs Partridge
characters.personality: wife who gives evidence at trial
characters.lifespan: appears as witness; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Mr Square
characters.personality: philosopher figure; rational and pedantic
characters.lifespan: offers educational opinions; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Mr Thwackum
characters.personality: divine/clerical, stern and doctrinal
characters.lifespan: opposes Mr Square in pedagogic debates; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Miss Sophia Western
characters.personality: beautiful, generous, romantic interest of Tom
characters.lifespan: suffers an accident; narrowly escapes other dangers; ultimate fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Squire Western
characters.personality: paternal, sometimes mad with passion for his daughter
characters.lifespan: active as magistrate and father; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Mrs Western
characters.personality: worldly, learned, politically and socially savvy
characters.lifespan: advises and guides; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Mrs Honour
characters.personality: scheming aunt who deploys stratagems
characters.lifespan: orchestrates plots involving Sophia; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Molly Seagrim
characters.personality: in perilous circumstances; vulnerable
characters.lifespan: has a narrow escape (Book IV); further fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Mr Supple
characters.personality: storyteller; offers narrative anecdotes
characters.lifespan: appears as narrator of a tale; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Mrs Waters
characters.personality: victim rescued by Jones
characters.lifespan: rescued from distress by Jones (Book IX); further fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Mr Benjamin
characters.personality: talented, comic figure associated with barber anecdotes
characters.lifespan: provides comic interludes; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: The Landlady
characters.personality: shrewd innkeeper, capable of address and schemes
characters.lifespan: manages inn adventures; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: The Barber (barber of Bagdad reference)

characters.lifespan: introduced as a notable barber; fate not specified in excerpt

characters.name: Lieutenant (unnamed)

unusual_words.definition: already listed; medical operator

unusual_words.definition: already listed; surgical procedures
unusual_words.context: Again referenced in descriptions of medical treatment.

unusual_words.definition: habitual intoxication
unusual_words.context: Book V chapter ix discusses drunkenness showing the mind of a man 'as a mirrour reflects his person'.

unusual_words.definition: archaic spelling of 'mirror'
unusual_words.context: Quoted simile about drunkenness in Book V chapter ix uses 'mirrour'.

unusual_words.definition: ancient Greek orator referenced by the author
unusual_words.context: Book V chapter xi cites Aeschines on drunkenness and human character.

unusual_words.context: Book V chapter x shows truth of observations of Ovid regarding wine and incontinency.

unusual_words.definition: incapable of being wrong
unusual_words.context: Book II claims a receipt 'which hath never been known to fail' (infallible) in reconciliation.

unusual_words.context: Book II chapter ix offers a 'proof' of the receipt's efficacy.

unusual_words.definition: singular of lamentations; expression of grief
unusual_words.context: Used describing the widow's grief in Book II.

unusual_words.definition: plural of receipt; here meaning remedies or recipes
unusual_words.context: Multiple 'receipts' for regaining affection or other aims are discussed.

unusual_words.definition: comfort or consolation
unusual_words.context: Chapters aim to 'relieve tender affections' and provide solace (Book VI).

unusual_words.definition: state of mind in a moment; disposition
unusual_words.context: Various scenes depict tempers in discord or reconciliation (multiple chapters).

unusual_words.definition: ceremony or splendor
unusual_words.context: Occasional satirical references to courtly pomp and critics appear.

unusual_words.definition: literary genre using humor to criticize
unusual_words.context: Author uses satire of critics, law, and social customs throughout the work.

unusual_words.definition: overly concerned with minor details or rules
unusual_words.context: Implied in depictions of Mr Square and educational disputes.
